title: Sphaerocionium molle
taxonomy: Sphaerocionium molle is classified as a species of liverwort, which is a group of non-vascular, simple plants that belong to the division Marchantiophyta. It is a member of the family Sphaerocionitaceae. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics. The species Sphaerocionium molle is also sometimes referred to as a type of thallose liverwort. Sphaerocionium molle is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world.
uses: Sphaerocionium molle is a species of lichen that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. intro: Sphaerocionium molle, also known as the soft hair cap, is a species of liverwort, a small, non-vascular plant that belongs to the class Marchantiopsida. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
